messages.author: Alfons Koenig
messages.text: Since a couple of weeks i am trying zo modify the firmware with platformIO. Who can help or give some hints? Or maybe a final firmware? Thanks very much in advance!

messages.author: diving1990
messages.text: I did managed to modify the files for my setup. esp32 s3 n16r8 with a dxlr30 which is a sx1262.
messages.content_html: I did managed to modify the files for my setup. esp32 s3 n16r8 with a dxlr30 which is a sx1262.

messages.author: Alfons Koenig
messages.text: Hey Diver, Thanks a lot for your effort. I hope i could add 2 pictures. It is a module bought at Ali. I am not sure about the right naming. Does this help? Thx, AK

messages.author: diving1990
messages.text: Hi Alfons, If i can see it is a bit confusing, There are two type numbers on your board, The one is XL1276 and the other XL1278 so i have to know which type you order. because i need that info for the custom setup for your board. My other question is, Do you really know shore that the esp32 s3 is a N18R8?
messages.content_html: Hi Alfons, If i can see it is a bit confusing, There are two type numbers on your board, The one is XL1276 and the other XL1278 so i have to know which type you order. because i need that info for the custom setup for your board. My other question is, Do you really know shore that the esp32 s3 is a N18R8?

messages.author: diving1990
messages.text: And can you show me your esp32 s3 board type? picture Thanks (edited)
messages.content_html: And can you show me your esp32 s3 board type? picture Thanks (edited)

messages.author: Alfons Koenig
diving1990
messages.text: Alfons, Thanks for the pictures, So you have the same ESP32 S3 N16R8 as i have. I did figured out that your Lora module has to be a XL1276 because that is the 868mhz version. So what i gonna do is to modify the files for my board and after that you can test your setup. Later on i give you the exact pin out connections to connect the Lora module with the ESP32 S3 (edited)
messages.content_html: Alfons, Thanks for the pictures, So you have the same ESP32 S3 N16R8 as i have. I did figured out that your Lora module has to be a XL1276 because that is the 868mhz version. So what i gonna do is to modify the files for my board and after that you can test your setup. Later on i give you the exact pin out connections to connect the Lora module with the ESP32 S3 (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Alfons Koenig
messages.text: Hi, I appriciate your effort so much! ThX! I am curious how you do it, because I put so much in the study of PlatformIO and the Code, to understand the coding of the firmware. I am looking forward to hear from you

messages.author: diving1990
messages.text: Hi Alfons, No problem. I like to help others. I did some test compilation for your board and the only thing i have to look at is the Busy pin that the sx1262 have and the XL1276 not. So i need to know how the function for Busy is. But i think i figure that out. An when the custom setup is ready, I will send you the files than you can look at the custom files to understand how you can customize other board combination in the future.
messages.content_html: Hi Alfons, No problem. I like to help others. I did some test compilation for your board and the only thing i have to look at is the Busy pin that the sx1262 have and the XL1276 not. So i need to know how the function for Busy is. But i think i figure that out. An when the custom setup is ready, I will send you the files than you can look at the custom files to understand how you can customize other board combination in the future.

messages.author: Alfons Koenig
messages.text: https://www.thethingsnetwork.org/forum/t/sx1262-applications/28685/20 Does that help: A gut Namen Graz says, there is noch Busy pin for SX1276. He uses: for SX1276 .dio is [DIO0, DIO1] for SX1262 .dio is [BUSY, DIO1] (edited)
messages.content_html: https://www.thethingsnetwork.org/forum/t/sx1262-applications/28685/20 Does that help: A gut Namen Graz says, there is noch Busy pin for SX1276. He uses: for SX1276 .dio is [DIO0, DIO1] for SX1262 .dio is [BUSY, DIO1] (edited)
embeds_html:
SX1262 Applications
Hi, As @LoRaTracker say DIO0 from SX1276 and BUSY from SX1262 has nothing in common. SX1262 do not have DIO0 and you can program the chip to send all events on DIO1 (this is simpler than SX1271) But SX1262 has a BUSY pin that the SX1276 do not have. The code need to know connection of BUSY pin and DIO1 pin. I reuse the same struct for the ne...
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: diving1990
messages.text: Here you can download the custom files for your setup. Just copy the board and variants folder to your work directory and overwrite them. I have added two images, The one is the pins from the Lora to the ESP and the other image is where you can chose the right environment you need like repeater or pompanion. At my platformio it compiles without any problem but only an warning and that you can ignore. https://limewire.com/d/SWspZ#Hq2nm3R3wf
messages.content_html: Here you can download the custom files for your setup. Just copy the board and variants folder to your work directory and overwrite them. I have added two images, The one is the pins from the Lora to the ESP and the other image is where you can chose the right environment you need like repeater or pompanion. At my platformio it compiles without any problem but only an warning and that you can ignore. https://limewire.com/d/SWspZ#Hq2nm3R3wf

messages.author: Alfons Koenig
messages.text: Hello, first of all: Thank you! It works. It took me 2-3 attemps for the companion, but it seems to work. Only sometimes I can send and receive messages. I assume the antenna is a crap. I will play with it and study the code. Maybe I can write something for an room server by myself. I have no clue which pin "RADIOLIB_NC" could be, so i let it. It works anyway. Which pin should I choose? AK (edited)

messages.text: Hello, I set up two nodes as compagnions. Both can be connected by bluetooth. They can send, but not receive. One has the spiralantenna. The otherone got a SMA antenna. I doublechecked the Wirsing and soldering. Do you have an idea? (edited)

messages.author: Alfons Koenig
messages.text: Hello "Diver", I found only a litte time to play around with the Lora-Modules. I went a step back and set up both equal and found sketches for the arduino ide to play around. My aim was to find out, if the modules work. They do - but with dropout. Since then I found no further time to work on the project. I would like to thank you on this way for your huge help, which I REALLY appriciate! Thank you! If I proceed - hopefully with success - I will inform here. Aim is to share it with everybody. Take care! All the best to you! AK
messages.content_html: Hello "Diver", I found only a litte time to play around with the Lora-Modules. I went a step back and set up both equal and found sketches for the arduino ide to play around. My aim was to find out, if the modules work. They do - but with dropout. Since then I found no further time to work on the project. I would like to thank you on this way for your huge help, which I REALLY appriciate! Thank you! If I proceed - hopefully with success - I will inform here. Aim is to share it with everybody.
